approach: Retire the two-persona mode-switch (ADR-0022 decision 8). Operator's command-center seat is asserted by invoking the skill — no runtime `cyberfleet mode` probe, no deferral to Pod. In-ship work still reaches Pod by topical routing.

Mode becomes Pod's own precondition: marker absent -> refuse the work, ask the Council to commission, on yes run `cyberfleet init` then carry on with the original request; on no write nothing. Pod never hands off to Operator.

Correct corpus-wide marker drift to `.agents/cyberfleet/ship.json` (the code was always right; `.cyberfleet/`, `.cyberfleet/config.json` and `.agents/cyberlegion/config.json` were all wrong — ADR-0022 decision 8 is the source of the last one).

No code change: `packages/cyberfleet/src/{mode,init}.ts` and `packages/cyberfleet/.agents/spec/**` already correct and untouched. `cyberfleet mode` the command survives; only its caller set shrinks to Pod.
Record as an `## Amendment` on ADR-0022 in place — its naming and query-first decisions stand.
why: leash auto-none: the CR deletes two acceptance scenarios from a @frozen suite, which fires the Clearance hard floor (not self-assertable), and re-opens six more. The owner (unional) holds the user channel live this session and directed the design, so every gate verdict is human-ratified rather than agent-self-asserted.
